title: pellaea calomelanos var. leucomelas
intro: Pellaea calomelanos var. leucomelas, also known as the leucomelan fern or golden shield fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pellaea calomelanos var. leucomelas include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, tonics, and salves.
